Putting it all together, I can formulate a clear line of reasoning: The context says there are frequent complaints. The context identifies the cause of those complaints as the high-income families throwing noisy parties. Therefore, the high-income families are the cause of the disturbances. The question asks who causes disturbances, and the answer, based on the context, is the high-income families. So A is the correct answer.
